preview_text:
The model favors Uruguay heavily, assigning them a 65% win probability against Saudi Arabia's 11%. The gap reflects a substantial quality differential, leaving Saudi Arabia a clear underdog with limited margin for error.

The draw sits at 24%, making it the second-most-likely outcome. This relatively high draw probability suggests the match will not be entirely one-sided; Saudi Arabia retains enough structural possibility of a result to prevent the favorite from appearing overwhelming. The W/D/L spread is moderately wide rather than compressed, indicating Uruguay should be expected to control much of play but without near-certainty of victory.

Model and market forecasts are closely aligned. The sportsbook consensus places Uruguay at 67% and Saudi Arabia at 12%, margins of 1–2 percentage points from the model across all outcomes. Polymarket pricing shows near-identical agreement. This convergence suggests broad consensus on the likely shape of the match.
